settings.title: Your Cold-Snap Checklist for Frozen Pipes
settings.excerpt: A short list of jobs to do before the first hard frost, while it is still easy to do them.
settings.body:
The coldest week of the year is when our phones never stop, and most of those calls were preventable in autumn. Here is what we tell our own families to do.

  • Lag any pipe in a loft, garage, or against an outside wall.
  • Know where your stopcock is and check it still turns.
  • Leave the heating ticking over low if you go away in a frost.
  • Disconnect and drain garden hoses and outside taps.

If a pipe does freeze, turn off the water and thaw it gently with a warm cloth, never a flame. A burst on thawing is the usual culprit behind a flooded kitchen.

A quick lagging job in November is a fraction of the cost of a January emergency.
